mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when applied outside mid-cycle expansion periods or when industrial production growth decouples from equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 58% of high-inflation trigger months or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate a positive risk-adjusted return with Sharpe ratio below 0.2 over 60-day horizons or shows negative correlation with forward returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals produce negative forward returns in greater than 50% of observations or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with accelerating inflation signals stagflationary pressures that erode corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0.1% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
failure_condition: The stagflation trigger fails to predict negative short-term returns with statistical significance or yields a positive return frequency exceeding 60% in out-of-sample tests.
